content:
# Judges Chapter 8 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), c. 1008 CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/JDG_page_276.jpg, JDG_page_277.jpg

1. And the men of Ephrayim said to him, "What [is] this thing you have done to us, not calling us when you went to fight Midyan?" And they quarreled with him fiercely.

2. And he said to them, "What have I done now compared to you? Are not the gleanings of Ephrayim better than the vintage of Avi-ezer?

3. Into your hand God gave the princes of Midyan — Orev and Ze'ev. And what was I able to do compared to you?" Then their spirit relaxed from upon him when he spoke this word.

4. And Gid'on came to the Yarden, crossing over — he and the three hundred men who [were] with him — exhausted but pursuing.

5. And he said to the men of Sukkot, "Please give loaves of bread to the people who [are] at my feet, for they [are] exhausted, and I [am] pursuing after Zevach and Tsalmunna, kings of Midyan."

6. And the officials of Sukkot said, "Is the palm of Zevach and Tsalmunna now in your hand, that we should give bread to your army?"

7. And Gid'on said, "Therefore, when YHWH gives Zevach and Tsalmunna into my hand, I will thresh your flesh with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers."

8. And he went up from there to P'nu'el and spoke to them likewise. And the men of P'nu'el answered him as the men of Sukkot had answered.

9. And he said also to the men of P'nu'el, "When I return in peace, I will tear down this tower."

10. And Zevach and Tsalmunna [were] in Qarqor, and their camps with them — about fifteen thousand — all who were left of all the camp of the sons of the east. And the fallen [were] a hundred and twenty thousand men who drew the sword.

11. And Gid'on went up by the way of the tent-dwellers, east of Novach and Yogb'hah, and he struck the camp while the camp was unsuspecting.

12. And Zevach and Tsalmunna fled, and he pursued after them. And he captured the two kings of Midyan — Zevach and Tsalmunna — and all the camp he threw into terror.

13. And Gid'on son of Yo'ash returned from the battle, from the ascent of Cheres.

14. And he captured a young man from the men of Sukkot and questioned him. And he wrote down for him the officials of Sukkot and its elders — seventy-seven men.

15. And he came to the men of Sukkot and said, "Behold Zevach and Tsalmunna, about whom you taunted me, saying, 'Is the palm of Zevach and Tsalmunna now in your hand, that we should give bread to your men who are exhausted?'"

16. And he took the elders of the city, and the thorns of the wilderness and the briers, and with them he taught the men of Sukkot.

17. And the tower of P'nu'el he tore down, and he killed the men of the city.

18. And he said to Zevach and Tsalmunna, "Where [are] the men whom you killed at Tavor?" And they said, "As you [are], so [were] they — each one [had] the form of sons of the king."

19. And he said, "They [were] my brothers, the sons of my mother. [As] YHWH lives, if you had kept them alive, I would not kill you."

20. And he said to Yeter his firstborn, "Arise, kill them." But the youth did not draw his sword, for he was afraid, for he was still a youth.

21. And Zevach and Tsalmunna said, "You arise and fall upon us, for as the man, so [is] his strength." And Gid'on arose and killed Zevach and Tsalmunna, and he took the crescent ornaments that [were] on the necks of their camels.

22. And the men of Yisrael said to Gid'on, "Rule over us — you and your son and your son's son — for you have saved us from the hand of Midyan."

23. And Gid'on said to them, "I will not rule over you, and my son will not rule over you. YHWH will rule over you."

24. And Gid'on said to them, "Let me make a request of you: give me each man the ring of his spoil." For they had rings of gold, because they [were] Yishma'elites.

25. And they said, "We will surely give." And they spread out a garment, and each man threw into it the ring of his spoil.

26. And the weight of the rings of gold that he requested was one thousand seven hundred [shekels] of gold, besides the crescent ornaments and the pendants and the purple garments that [were] on the kings of Midyan, and besides the chains that [were] on the necks of their camels.

27. And Gid'on made it into an ephod and set it up in his city, in Ophrah. And all Yisrael went whoring after it there, and it became a snare to Gid'on and to his house.

28. And Midyan was subdued before the sons of Yisrael, and they did not again lift their head. And the land had rest forty years in the days of Gid'on.

29. And Yerubba'al son of Yo'ash went and dwelt in his house.

30. And Gid'on had seventy sons going out from his thigh, for many wives he had.

31. And his concubine who [was] in Sh'khem — she also bore him a son. And he set his name Avimelekh.

32. And Gid'on son of Yo'ash died at a good old age, and he was buried in the tomb of Yo'ash his father, in Ophrah of the Avi-ezrites.

33. And it was, when Gid'on died, the sons of Yisrael turned back and went whoring after the Ba'als. And they set for themselves Ba'al-B'rit as god.

34. And the sons of Yisrael did not remember YHWH their God, who had delivered them from the hand of all their enemies all around.

35. And they did not show loyalty to the house of Yerubba'al — [that is,] Gid'on — for all the good that he had done for Yisrael.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.16 — "he taught the men of Sukkot":** vayyoda bahem — literally "he made known with them" or "he taught with them [the thorns]." A grim euphemism for torture or execution. The verb yada' ("to know/make known") is used ironically — he made them "know" through pain.

2. **v.27 — "went whoring after it":** vayyiznu acharav — the same prostitution metaphor used for idolatry throughout Judges. The ephod — originally a priestly garment — becomes an object of worship, and Gideon, who refused kingship, creates an idol.

3. **v.30 — "going out from his thigh":** yots'ei y'rekho — "going out from his thigh/loins." A standard Hebrew idiom for biological offspring.

— EMET, AI Translator
